title: China in 26: Europe-China relations + Russian-Chinese military cooperation + new economic data
gist: A MERICS podcast episode frames three near-term China watch items as open questions: whether German/EU-China relations are heading toward cooperation or confrontation, whether recent China-Russia military maneuvers are a symbolic show of force or an underappreciated threat, and what China's newest growth data imply for Xi Jinping's push to build a stronger domestic economy.

insights.claim: MERICS analysts are treating the current state of German and European relations with China as genuinely unresolved, framing the near-term path as a binary between deeper cooperation and outright confrontation rather than assuming continuity.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Investors exposed to EU-China trade and industrial supply chains should watch for near-term signals (policy statements, trade measures) that resolve this cooperation-vs-confrontation question rather than assuming the status quo holds.
insights.quote: Are we headed toward cooperation or confrontation?
insights.locator: First, what is the state of German and European relations with China?

insights.claim: MERICS points to its companion piece on the EU needing to confront China's trade challenge as directly relevant background to the cooperation-versus-confrontation question being debated on this episode.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Signals an EU policy stance that is hardening toward China on trade, worth tracking for tariff/investment-screening risk in EU-exposed China supply chains.
insights.locator: You can learn more about the topics in this episode here

insights.claim: MERICS is explicitly weighing whether recent joint Chinese-Russian military maneuvers should be read as mostly symbolic posturing or as an underappreciated strategic threat, indicating internal debate among China-watchers about how seriously to take the deepening military relationship.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Escalating China-Russia military coordination is a tail-risk factor for geopolitical risk premia and sanctions exposure; MERICS' own China-Russia Dashboard is flagged as the tool to monitor it.
insights.quote: Are they a show of force or an overlooked threat?
insights.locator: Second: How should we interpret the latest military maneuvers by China and Russia?

insights.claim: MERICS maintains a dedicated 'China-Russia Dashboard' tracking facts and figures on the China-Russia relationship, treated as the reference resource for assessing the trajectory of their military and strategic ties.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: A dedicated tracking tool signals MERICS sees the China-Russia relationship as a persistent, data-monitorable risk factor rather than a one-off event.
insights.locator: You can learn more about the topics in this episode here

themes.theme: China growth data and Xi's domestic economy agenda

insights.claim: MERICS reads China's newly released growth figures primarily through the lens of what they imply for Xi Jinping's plans to build a strong domestic economy, treating the data as a test of that policy agenda rather than a standalone macro release.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Frames upcoming China GDP/growth prints as a proxy for whether Xi's domestic-economy-building strategy is on track, useful context for interpreting headline China data releases.
insights.quote: What do China’s latest growth figures mean for Xi Jinping’s plans to build a strong domestic economy?
insights.locator: And third: What do China's latest growth figures mean

insights.claim: MERICS links a separate briefing covering China's export surge and Huawei's 'Tau Scaling Law' alongside Sino-German trade, indicating these are flagged as connected developments worth tracking together with the domestic growth story.
insights.speaker: MERICS
insights.so_what: Huawei's 'Tau Scaling Law' is named as a notable China-tech development bundled with the export-surge narrative; worth a follow-up look for anyone tracking Chinese AI/chip capability claims.
insights.quote: China’s export surge + Huawei’s Tau Scaling Law + Sino-German trade – MERICS Europe China 360°
insights.locator: You can learn more about the topics in this episode here
